content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: My ___ is only six months old and sleeps most of the day.
distractor_rationale: a cousin is a relative of any age, not specifically someone who is six months old and sleeps most of the day
a bride is a woman on her wedding day, not an infant
an adult is a fully grown person, not a six-month-old

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: Tom's ___ is his mother's sister, and she visits us every summer.
distractor_rationale: a daughter is a female child of a parent, not a mother's sister
a cousin is the child of a parent's sibling, not the sibling herself
a bride is a woman getting married, not a family relation defined by being a mother's sister

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: Sara called her ___ to ask for advice because he is her mother's brother.
distractor_rationale: a dad is a father, not a mother's brother
a cousin is the child of a parent's sibling, not the sibling himself
a brother is a sibling of the same parents, not a mother's brother

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: After the ceremony, the ___ walked down the aisle in her white dress while everyone watched.
distractor_rationale: an aunt is a parent's sister and does not walk down the aisle in a white dress at a ceremony
a daughter is a female child and is not specifically the person in a white dress walking down the aisle at a wedding ceremony
a cousin is a relative who may attend a wedding but does not walk down the aisle in a white dress as the central figure

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: Anna and her ___ have been together for two years and recently started living in the same flat.
distractor_rationale: a brother is a sibling and would not be described as someone Anna has been 'together with' romantically for two years
a cousin is a family relative, not a romantic partner someone lives with after two years together
a dad is a father, not a romantic partner someone moves in with after dating

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The young ___ shared a bedroom with his older sister and they often argued over the light at night.
distractor_rationale: an adult is a fully grown person who would not typically share a childhood bedroom with a sister in this context
a couple refers to two people in a relationship, not a single young male child
a bride is a woman on her wedding day, not a young male child sharing a bedroom

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The elderly ___ held hands as they walked slowly through the park together every morning.
distractor_rationale: a family refers to a group of related people, not specifically two people holding hands as romantic partners
a child is a young person and cannot be described as elderly
a cousin is a single relative, not two people described together holding hands as a unit

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: After the ___, both parents agreed to share responsibility for their children and meet every week.
distractor_rationale: an engagement is the period before marriage and does not result in two parents needing to share child responsibility after a separation
a wedding is a marriage ceremony, which joins two people together rather than separating them into shared custody arrangements
a reunion is when people come back together after being apart, the opposite of a separation requiring custody arrangements

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: Mark and Lisa announced they were ___ at dinner last night and showed everyone the ring she was wearing.
distractor_rationale: divorced means a marriage has legally ended, which contradicts the celebratory announcement and the ring being shown
related means connected by family ties, not a status announced at dinner with a ring as evidence
adopted refers to a legal process of becoming someone's child, not a romantic commitment announced with a ring

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: As the only ___ in the room, David was asked to supervise the younger children during the school trip.
distractor_rationale: a cousin is a family relative and would not be specifically identified as the responsible supervisor in contrast to younger children
a boy is a young male child and cannot supervise other children as a responsible authority figure
a baby is an infant who cannot supervise anyone or take on any responsibility

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: My ___ is only six months old, so she sleeps a lot during the day.
distractor_rationale: a cousin is a relative of any age, not specifically an infant who sleeps all day
an aunt is a parent's sister, not a six-month-old child
a bride is a woman on her wedding day, not a six-month-old infant

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: Tom's ___ is his father's sister, and she visits the family every summer.
distractor_rationale: a daughter is a parent's female child, not a father's sister
a bride is a woman getting married, not a family relative defined by a sibling relationship
a cousin is the child of a parent's sibling, not the sibling herself

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The ___ wore a long white dress and carried flowers as she walked down the aisle at the church.
distractor_rationale: a daughter is defined by her relationship to a parent, not by wearing a wedding dress and walking down an aisle
a cousin is a family relative, not a role specifically associated with a wedding ceremony and white dress
an aunt is a parent's sister, not a person whose role involves walking down a church aisle in a white dress

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: After their ___, Maria and her husband sold their shared apartment and each moved to a different city.
distractor_rationale: an engagement is when two people decide to get married, which would not cause them to separate and sell their shared home
couple is a noun referring to two people together, not an event that results in selling a shared home and moving apart
a baby is a newborn child, not an event that causes two people to sell their apartment and live in separate cities

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: Sara and James announced they were ___ at dinner last night, and Sara showed everyone the ring James had given her.
distractor_rationale: divorced means a marriage has legally ended, which contradicts showing a new ring as a symbol of a future commitment
adult describes a person who is fully grown, not a relationship status announced at dinner with a ring
coupled is not a standard English adjective used to describe a relationship status announcement, and showing a ring specifically signals an engagement

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: My mother's sister is my ___.
distractor_rationale: a cousin is the child of a parent's sibling, not the sibling herself
a niece is the daughter of one's sibling, not the sibling of a parent
a bride is a woman getting married, not a family relationship term

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: Tom and Sara have a new ___ — she was born just three days ago.
distractor_rationale: boy refers to a male child, but the sentence says 'she', contradicting 'boy'
an adult is a fully grown person, not someone born three days ago
a couple refers to two people in a relationship, not a newborn child

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: Anna has one ___ — his name is James and he is two years older than her.
distractor_rationale: a cousin is not a direct sibling, and the sentence implies a sibling relationship with 'older than her'
dad refers to a father, not a sibling who is two years older
a boyfriend is a romantic partner, not a family sibling

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: Maria and her ___ have been together for two years and they share a flat in the city centre.
distractor_rationale: sharing a flat as a romantic couple does not describe a cousin relationship
a brother is a sibling, not a romantic partner one shares a flat with as a couple
a dad is a father figure, not a romantic partner

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: Every ___ in the class brought a drawing they had made at home to show the teacher.
distractor_rationale: adults do not typically bring drawings to show a teacher in a school class context
a couple refers to two people in a relationship, not individuals in a school class
a bride is a woman getting married, not a student in a class

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The ___ wore a long white dress and walked slowly into the ceremony hall.
distractor_rationale: an aunt is a family member but does not specifically wear a white dress for a ceremony
a daughter is a family role that does not specifically involve a white dress at a ceremony
a cousin is a family member but the white dress and ceremony hall specifically describe a bride

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: David's parents got ___ when he was ten, and after that he lived with his mother.
distractor_rationale: getting engaged means planning to marry, which contradicts living apart after the event
getting married means starting a life together, not splitting up and living separately
separated is close in meaning but 'divorced' is the correct legal term and the only one from the target vocabulary list that fits the formal ending of a marriage

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: Lisa and Mark announced last night that they are ___ and plan to get married next summer.
distractor_rationale: divorced means the marriage has ended, which contradicts planning to get married
if they were already married, they would not be planning to get married next summer
being related describes a family connection by blood, not a formal commitment to marry

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: My uncle's son, who grew up in another city, is my ___, and we only see each other at family gatherings.
distractor_rationale: a nephew is the son of one's sibling, not the son of one's uncle
a brother is a direct sibling who shares the same parents, not the son of an uncle
an aunt is a female relative, not a male cousin described as 'son' and 'he'

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: James is proud that his ___ just graduated from university with top marks, as she is the first in the family to do so.
distractor_rationale: an aunt is a parent's sister, not someone whose graduation would make a parent proud in this direct way
a bride is a woman getting married, not someone graduating from university
a cousin is a more distant relative, and the phrase 'first in the family' combined with parental pride strongly implies a direct parent-child relationship

content.instruction: Use the word in brackets in the correct form to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: My little sister is only three years old, so she is still a ___. (BABY)

content.instruction: Use the word in brackets in the correct form to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: He is only twelve, so he is still a ___. (CHILD)

content.instruction: Use the word in brackets in the correct form to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: Tom and Anna have been together for two years, and everyone says they make a lovely ___. (COUPLE)

content.instruction: Use the word in brackets in the correct form to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: After many years of problems in their marriage, Maria's parents decided to get a ___. (DIVORCE)

content.instruction: Use the word in brackets in the correct form to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: By the time you turn eighteen, you are considered an ___ and can make your own decisions. (ADULT)
